Campaign.title: Campaign
Campaign.description: Represents a D&D campaign, tracking its overall progress and settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Campaign entity.

description.description: A brief summary or description of the campaign's storyline and goals.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the campaign was created.

Character.title: Character
Character.description: Represents a player character in a campaign.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Character entity.

species.description: The character's species (e.g., Human, Elf, Dwarf).

class.description: The character's class (e.g., Fighter, Wizard, Rogue).

backstory.description: The character's backstory, as provided by the player or DM.

developmentLog.description: A log of the character's development and significant moments, often updated by AI analysis.

Npc.title: Npc
Npc.description: Represents a non-player character (NPC) in a campaign.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

description.description: A description of the NPC's appearance, personality, and backstory.

location.description: The location where the NPC was first encountered or resides.

Location.title: Location
Location.description: Represents a key location within a campaign world.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Location entity.

description.description: A description of the location, including its appearance, history, and key features.

Session.title: Session
Session.description: Represents a single D&D session within a campaign.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Session entity.

sessionNumber.description: The sequential number of the session within the campaign.

summary.description: A brief summary of the events that transpired during the session.

aiSummary.description: A concise, AI-generated summary of the session.

PlotHook.title: PlotHook
PlotHook.description: Represents a plot hook generated for the campaign.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlotHook entity.

description.description: The description of the plot hook.

sessionId.description: Reference to Session. (Relationship: Session 1:N PlotHook). The session this plothook originated from.

EncounterIdea.title: EncounterIdea
EncounterIdea.description: Represents an encounter idea generated for the campaign.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EncounterIdea entity.

description.description: The description of the encounter idea.

sessionId.description: Reference to Session. (Relationship: Session 1:N EncounterIdea). The session this encounter idea originated from.

NpcConcept.title: NpcConcept
NpcConcept.description: Represents an NPC concept generated for the campaign.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NpcConcept entity.

description.description: The description of the NPC concept.

sessionId.description: Reference to Session. (Relationship: Session 1:N NpcConcept). The session this NPC concept originated from.

SavedConcept.title: SavedConcept
SavedConcept.description: Represents an idea saved by the user to their library.

userId.description: The ID of the user who owns this concept.

content.description: The main content of the saved idea.

context.description: Optional context about why this idea was generated.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the concept was saved.

definition.description: Stores campaign data for each user. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the campaign.

params.name: campaignId
params.description: The ID of the campaign.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability for the Grimlore Forge application. It follows a hierarchical path structure, starting with users and branching into campaigns, sessions, and related entities. This structure enables simple, robust, and easily debuggable security rules.

**Authorization Independence:** Each subcollection includes the necessary authorization context, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. For example, the `/users/{userId}/campaigns/{campaignId}/sessions/{sessionId}` path ensures that each session is associated with a campaign and user, and authorization can be determined based on the document's data without needing to traverse up the hierarchy.

**Structural Segregation:** The structure segregates data based on ownership and access needs. User-owned campaigns are stored under `/users/{userId}/campaigns/{campaignId}`, while sessions, party decisions, plot hooks, encounter ideas, and NPC concepts are nested under their respective campaigns. This segregation simplifies security rules by ensuring that all documents within a collection share the same security requirements.

**Access Modeling:** The structure uses path-based ownership for private user data and hierarchical paths for user-owned entities. Campaigns are owned by users (`/users/{userId}/campaigns/{campaignId}`), sessions are associated with campaigns (`/users/{userId}/campaigns/{campaignId}/sessions/{sessionId}`), and related entities are nested under sessions or campaigns. This model ensures clear ownership and simplifies access control.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure supports secure `list` operations by leveraging the hierarchical structure and path-based ownership. Security rules can easily filter lists based on user ID and campaign ID, ensuring that users can only access data they own or have explicit permission to view.
